perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information” under this Agreement shall not include any information that (i) is or becomes publicly available through no wrongful act of Recipient; (ii) was known by Recipient prior to disclosure by Discloser, as evidenced by tangible records; (iii) becomes known to Recipient after disclosure from a third party having an apparent bona fide right to disclose it without any confidentiality obligation; (iv) is independently developed or discovered by Recipient without use of Discloser’s Confidential Information, as evidenced by tangible records; or (v) is disclosed to another party by Discloser without restriction on further disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information” under this Agreement shall not include any information that (i) is or becomes publicly available through no wrongful act of Recipient; (ii) was known by Recipient prior to disclosure by Discloser, as evidenced by tangible records; (iii) becomes known to Recipient after disclosure from a third party having an apparent bona fide right to disclose it without any confidentiality obligation; (iv) is independently developed or discovered by Recipient without use of Discloser’s Confidential Information, as evidenced by tangible records; or (v) is disclosed to another party by Discloser without restriction on further disclosure. **However, any information disclosed due to legal requirements is still considered Confidential Information.**
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception to the exclusions of Confidential Information, stating that legally required disclosures are still considered Confidential. This directly contradicts exclusion (v), which states that information disclosed to another party without restriction on further disclosure is *not* Confidential Information. This contradiction occurs within section 2 itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Exclusions . “Confidential Information” under this Agreement shall not include any information that (i) is or becomes publicly available through no wrongful act of Recipient; (ii) was known by Recipient prior to disclosure by Discloser, as evidenced by tangible records; (iii) becomes known to Recipient after disclosure from a third party having an apparent bona fide right to disclose it without any confidentiality obligation; (iv) is independently developed or discovered by Recipient without use of Discloser’s Confidential Information, as evidenced by tangible records; or (v) is disclosed to another party by Discloser without restriction on further disclosure. However, any information disclosed due to legal requirements is still considered Confidential Information. The obligations of confidentiality and non-use set forth in this Agreement shall not apply with respect to any information that Recipient is required to disclose or produce pursuant to applicable law, court order or other valid legal process provided that Recipient promptly notifies Discloser prior to such required disclosure, discloses such information only to the extent so required and cooperates reasonably with Discloser’s efforts to contest or limit the scope of such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception stating that disclosures due to legal requirements are still considered Confidential Information. This directly contradicts exclusion (v) in both the original and contradicted text (which are actually the same here), which states that information disclosed to another party without restriction on further disclosure is *not* Confidential Information. The added clause creates a direct conflict with this exclusion, leading to uncertainty about whether legally required disclosures are confidential or not. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' where key terms are defined inconsistently, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may, however, disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only on a need-to-know basis to its and its Affiliates employees, staff members and agents (“Receiving Individuals”) who are directly participating in the Purpose and who are informed of the confidential nature of such information, provided Recipient shall be responsible for compliance by Receiving Individuals with the terms of this Agreement and any breach thereof
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may, however, disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only on a need-to-know basis to its and its Affiliates employees, staff members and agents (“Receiving Individuals”) who are directly participating in the Purpose and who are informed of the confidential nature of such information, provided Recipient shall be responsible for compliance by Receiving Individuals with the terms of this Agreement and any breach thereof. **However, Recipient can disclose Confidential Information to Receiving Individuals without informing them of its confidential nature, if it is necessary for the Purpose.**
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the previous condition that Receiving Individuals must be informed of the confidential nature of the information. The added clause allows disclosure without this condition, creating a direct contradiction within the same section (Section 4).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Restrictions . For the term of the License Agreement and a period of [***] thereafter (and indefinitely with respect to any individually identifiable health information disclosed by BIDMC to Licensee, if any), each Recipient agrees that: (i) it will not use such Confidential Information for any purpose other than as specified herein; and (ii) it will use reasonable efforts (but no less than the efforts used to protect its own confidential and/or proprietary information of a similar nature) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any other person or entity except as expressly permitted hereunder or the License Agreement. Recipient may, however, disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only on a need-to-know basis to its and its Affiliates employees, staff members and agents (“Receiving Individuals”) who are directly participating in the Purpose and who are informed of the confidential nature of such information, provided Recipient shall be responsible for compliance by Receiving Individuals with the terms of this Agreement and any breach thereof Each party further agrees not to use the name of the other party or any of its Affiliates or any of their respective trustees, directors, officers, staff members, 19 [***] = CERTAIN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION CONTAINED IN THIS DOCUMENT, MARKED BY BRACKETS, HAS BEEN OMITTED BECAUSE THE INFORMATION (I) IS NOT MATERIAL AND (II) WOULD BE COMPETITIVELY HARMFUL IF PUBLICLY DISCLOSED. employees, students or agents in any advertising, promotional or sales literature, publicity or in any document employed to obtain funds or financing without the prior written approval of the party or individual whose name is to be used, in the case of BIDMC such approval to be given by the Public Affairs Department. This Section 4 shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement. However, Recipient can disclose Confidential Information to Receiving Individuals without informing them of its confidential nature, if it is necessary for the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that the Recipient can disclose Confidential Information to Receiving Individuals without informing them of its confidential nature, if it is necessary for the Purpose. This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text (which are the same in this case), which states that Recipient may disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information only to Receiving Individuals who are informed of the confidential nature of such information. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency within the agreement, as it allows for disclosure under conditions explicitly prohibited in the preceding sentence.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, if requested by Discloser, Recipient shall return or destroy at Discloser’s discretion all of Discloser’s Confidential Information, provided that Recipient shall be entitled to keep one copy of such Confidential Information in a secure location solely for the purpose of determining Recipient’s legal obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, if requested by Discloser, Recipient shall return or destroy at Discloser’s discretion all of Discloser’s Confidential Information, provided that Recipient shall be entitled to keep one copy of such Confidential Information in a secure location solely for the purpose of determining Recipient’s legal obligations hereunder. **However, the Recipient is obligated to keep all Confidential Information and cannot return nor destroy it, due to regulations.**
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the established procedure for handling confidential information upon termination. It states that the Recipient *must* keep all information and cannot return or destroy it, overriding the Discloser's discretion. This creates an in-text contradiction in section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Ownership . All Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement, including without limitation all written and tangible forms thereof, shall be and remain the property of the Discloser. Upon termination of this Agreement, if requested by Discloser, Recipient shall return or destroy at Discloser’s discretion all of Discloser’s Confidential Information, provided that Recipient shall be entitled to keep one copy of such Confidential Information in a secure location solely for the purpose of determining Recipient’s legal obligations hereunder. However, the Recipient is obligated to keep all Confidential Information and cannot return nor destroy it, due to regulations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the Discloser's right to request the return or destruction of confidential information upon termination of the agreement, as stated in both the original and contradicted text. The addition of the clause mandating the Recipient to keep all confidential information, regardless of the Discloser's request, creates a clear and irreconcilable conflict. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because it introduces inconsistent obligations regarding the handling of confidential information, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Each party acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement by it may cause irreparable harm to the other party and that each party is entitled to seek injunctive relief and any other remedy available at law or in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement by it may cause irreparable harm to the other party and that each party is entitled to seek injunctive relief and any other remedy available at law or in equity. **However, the sole remedy for any breach of this agreement is limited to monetary damages, with no right to injunctive relief.**
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a limitation on the remedies available for breach of the agreement. The original clause allows for injunctive relief, while the changed clause explicitly denies it, limiting the remedy to monetary damages only. This creates an in-text contradiction within section 8.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Remedies . Each party acknowledges that any breach of this Agreement by it may cause irreparable harm to the other party and that each party is entitled to seek injunctive relief and any other remedy available at law or in equity. However, the sole remedy for any breach of this agreement is limited to monetary damages, with no right to injunctive relief.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly limits the remedy for a breach of agreement to monetary damages, denying the right to injunctive relief. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, contains the original clause allowing for injunctive relief, which is then immediately negated by the added limitation. This direct contradiction creates an in-text contradiction as described by the perturbation explanation.

perturbation.original_text: These Confidentiality Terms and Conditions, along with the License Agreement, contain the entire understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersede any prior oral or written understandings between the parties relating to confidential treatment of information.
perturbation.changed_text: These Confidentiality Terms and Conditions, along with the License Agreement, contain the entire understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersede any prior oral or written understandings between the parties relating to confidential treatment of information. **However, a verbal agreement made between the parties on January 1, 2017 regarding data access permissions shall take precedence over these terms.**
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the 'entire agreement' clause. The added clause states that a prior verbal agreement *does* take precedence, despite the original clause saying all prior understandings are superseded. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty in section 10, as it's now unclear which agreements govern the relationship.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. General . These Confidentiality Terms and Conditions, along with the License Agreement, contain the entire understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersede any prior oral or written understandings between the parties relating to confidential treatment of information. However, a verbal agreement made between the parties on January 1, 2017 regarding data access permissions shall take precedence over these terms. Sections 1, 2, 4, 7, 10 and 11 of these Confidentiality Terms and Conditions shall survive any expiration or termination of the License Agreement. 20 [***] = CERTAIN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION CONTAINED IN THIS DOCUMENT, MARKED BY BRACKETS, HAS BEEN OMITTED BECAUSE THE INFORMATION (I) IS NOT MATERIAL AND (II) WOULD BE COMPETITIVELY HARMFUL IF PUBLICLY DISCLOSED.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that a prior verbal agreement takes precedence over the confidentiality terms and conditions and the license agreement. This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, which both state that the agreement supersedes any prior oral or written understandings. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because the altered clause establishes an exception to the 'entire agreement' provision, making it unclear which agreement governs data access permissions.
